evals.name: design-system-component-button
evals.description: Validate that the skill produces a design system Button component with proper token usage, accessibility, interaction states, and composable API following component architecture best practices.
evals.input: Create a production-grade Button component for a design system. It must support variants (primary, secondary, ghost, danger), sizes (sm, md, lg), loading state with spinner, disabled state, icon support (leading and trailing), and full accessibility. Use design tokens for all visual values. Provide the component in React with TypeScript.

assertions.description: The Button must include ARIA attributes (aria-disabled, aria-busy, aria-label) to communicate state to assistive technologies beyond visual indicators.
Visual values must reference CSS custom properties (design tokens) rather than hardcoded hex colors, pixel values, or magic numbers.
The component must handle the disabled state both visually and functionally, preventing click events and conveying the state to screen readers.
The component must support a loading state that shows a spinner, disables interaction, and communicates the busy state via aria-busy.
The component must accept a variant prop to switch between visual styles (primary, secondary, ghost, danger) so that usage context drives appearance.

evals.name: accessible-form-pattern
evals.description: Validate that the skill produces an accessible form with proper label associations, validation feedback, keyboard navigation, error handling, and responsive layout.
evals.input: Create a registration form with fields for full name, email, password, and password confirmation. Include inline validation with accessible error messages, a password strength indicator, proper label associations, keyboard navigation support, and a responsive two-column layout that collapses to single column on mobile. Use semantic HTML and ARIA where needed. Implement in React with TypeScript.

assertions.description: Every form field must have a label element with htmlFor (or for) attribute matching the input id to create a programmatic association for screen readers.
Error messages must be linked to their input fields via aria-describedby so that screen readers announce the error when the field receives focus.
The form must use ARIA roles (alert, status, or progressbar) for dynamic content like error summaries or the password strength indicator.
Required fields must be marked with the required attribute or aria-required so that the requirement is communicated to both visual users and assistive technologies.
The form must handle submission with proper validation, preventing default form behavior and managing focus to the first error field on validation failure.
